validated.procedure_name: Criteria 2: % Total Body Surface Area Burns (TBSA): Upto 25%; Includes % TBSA skin grafted, flap cover, follow-up dressings etc. as deemed necessary; Surgical procedures are required for deep burns that are not amenable to heal with dressings alone.
stg_missing_criteria: Minimum doctor qualification (M.Ch / DNB/ equivalent in Plastic Surgery) not specified in the clinical presentation
validated.stg_reasoning: The patient presents with 25% Total Body Surface Area (TBSA) mixed 2nd and 3rd degree thermal burns. This TBSA is well within the 'Upto 40% TBSA' limit specified in the STG indication. The presence of 3rd degree burns and eschar formation on the right upper limb unequivocally indicates 'deep thermal burns'. Such deep burns, particularly full-thickness (3rd degree) and deep partial-thickness (deep 2nd degree) with eschar, are inherently 'requiring surgical procedures (skin grafting, flap cover) and not amenable to heal with dressings alone'. Therefore, the single clinical indication for BM001B is fully satisfied. No clinical thresholds are specified for this procedure. The minimum doctor qualification is an administrative criterion not explicitly stated in the patient's clinical presentation, which is noted as a missing criterion but does not preclude clinical eligibility.

blocked_candidates.message: The patient's clinical presentation indicates a total body surface area (TBSA) of 25% thermal burns. The sole clinical indication for procedure BM001C explicitly requires the patient to have 40% - 60% TBSA deep thermal burns. The patient's TBSA of 25% falls outside this mandatory numeric range, thus failing the primary eligibility criterion. While the burns are described as mixed 2nd and 3rd degree, consistent with 'deep thermal burns requiring surgical procedures', the TBSA percentage is a strict numeric boundary that is not met. The minimum doctor qualification is also not documented, but the TBSA mismatch is the definitive clinical disqualifier.
missing_criteria: Total Body Surface Area (TBSA) of burns not within the 40% - 60% range: patient has 25% TBSA burns, while the STG requires 40% - 60% TBSA
Minimum doctor qualification (M.Ch / DNB/ equivalent in Plastic Surgery) not documented
